events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide compliance. Scenario: backstabbed device on 20A circuit. TRD says legal but journeymen won't. NEC 110.14 requires terminals and splices suitable for material and ampacity, tightness, etc. Backstab (push-in) terminals on receptacles are generally allowed by UL listing for 14 AWG only, sometimes 12 AWG? Many backstab devices rated for 14 AWG only; on 20A circuit conductor likely 12 AWG. If backstabbed with 12 AWG, may exceed terminal rating -> non-compliant with 110.14 and manufacturer instructions. Also backstab connections are known failure, but code legality depends on listing. The prompt says TRD: legal but journeymen won't. But task says if dangerous or non-compliant, decision fail. Need assess. Since 20A circuit, conductor is 12 AWG. Backstab terminals on many devices are only listed for 14 AWG solid copper. Using on 12 AWG violates manufacturer instructions and NEC 110.14. Therefore fail. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk maybe high due to overheating/fire. Work stage rough-complete. percent_complete maybe 80. Findings: device terminated using backstab (push-in) terminal on 20A circuit; conductor likely 12 AWG exceeds terminal rating; poor reliability/heat. Actions: remove and reterminate using side screw terminals or approved connectors per manufacturer; verify torque. Rationale. References NEC 110.14, manufacturer instructions. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

Back-stab terminals on many devices are listed only for 14 AWG solid copper, so using them on a 20A circuit with 12 AWG conductors is non-compliant and creates a significant overheating/fire hazard.",
